 {"id":"bd-1ega.9","title":"Regenerate installer scripts from updated acfs.manifest.yaml","description":"## CRITICAL Task

After ALL manifest updates are complete, regenerate the installer scripts.

## Why This Is Critical

Per AGENTS.md, files in scripts/generated/ are AUTO-GENERATED from acfs.manifest.yaml.
Manual edits will be OVERWRITTEN. We must regenerate after manifest changes.

## Commands

```bash
cd packages/manifest
bun run generate
```

## Files Generated

- scripts/generated/install_*.sh
- scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh
- scripts/generated/manifest_index.sh

## Verification

```bash
shellcheck scripts/generated/*.sh
```

## Dependencies

This task should run AFTER all manifest.yaml updates but BEFORE E2E testing.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All generated scripts updated
2. shellcheck passes on generated scripts
3. +{"id":"bd-1lug","title":"Rate limit backoff for GitHub API calls","description":"## Overview
Implement exponential backoff for GitHub API rate limit errors to prevent installation failures during peak times.

## Current Problem
- GitHub API has rate limits (60/hr unauthenticated, 5000/hr with token)
- Installation fetches many resources (releases, checksums)
- Rate limit errors cause hard failures
- No retry mechanism

## Proposed Behavior
- Detect 403 rate limit responses
- Implement exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 60s
- Show user-friendly message during wait
- Fall back to cached versions if available
- Suggest `gh auth login` if unauthenticated

## Implementation Details
```bash
github_fetch_with_backoff() {
  local url="$1" max_retries=5 delay=1
  for ((i=1; i<=max_retries; i++)); do
    response=$(curl -sS -w '%{http_code}' -o /tmp/response "$url")
    if [[ "$response" == "200" ]]; then
      cat /tmp/response; return 0
    elif [[ "$response" == "403" ]]; then
      log_warn "Rate limited, waiting ${delay}s (attempt $i/$max_retries)"
      sleep "$delay"
      delay=$((delay * 2))
    else
      return 1
    fi
  done
  return 1
}
```

## Rate Limit Detection
- HTTP 403 with "rate limit" in body
- X-RateLimit-Remaining header = 0
- X-RateLimit-Reset header for exact wait time

## User Communication
```
⏳ GitHub rate limit reached. Waiting 8s before retry (3/5)...
💡 Tip: Run 'gh auth login' for higher rate limits
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test backoff timing is correct
- [ ] Test max retries respected
- [ ] Test successful retry after limit clears
- [ ] Mock test for rate limit response handling
- [ ] Test fallback to cached versions

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/security.sh (fetch_with_checksum uses curl)

+{"id":"bd-21kh","title":"Progress bar during tool installation","description":"## Overview
Add a visual progress bar during tool installation showing overall progress and current tool being installed.

## Current Problem
- Installation can take several minutes
- Users see scrolling text but no sense of overall progress
- No way to estimate time remaining
- Anxiety: "Is it stuck or still working?"

## Proposed UI
```
Installing ACFS tools...
[████████████░░░░░░░░] 12/26 tools (46%)
Current: Installing rust toolchain...
```

## Implementation Details
1. Count total tools to install upfront
2. Update progress after each tool completes
3. Show current tool name during install
4. Use ANSI escape codes for in-place updates
5. Respect NO_COLOR environment variable

## Progress Calculation
- Parse manifest.yaml for tool count
- Subtract already-installed tools
- Increment counter after each install_tool() call

## Terminal Handling
```bash
# In-place update (same line)
printf '\r[%-20s] %d/%d tools (%d%%)' "$bar" "$done" "$total" "$pct"

# With NO_COLOR or non-TTY
echo "[$done/$total] Installing $tool..."
```

## Graceful Degradation
- Non-interactive: Simple line-by-line output
- NO_COLOR: Text-only progress
- Narrow terminal: Abbreviated format

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test progress updates correctly
- [ ] Test NO_COLOR fallback
- [ ] Test non-TTY output (piped)
- [ ] Test narrow terminal handling
- [ ] Visual test: progress matches actual completion

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh (add progress tracking)
- scripts/install.sh (initialize progress bar)
- New: scripts/lib/progress.sh (progress bar utilities)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:45.195454182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:07:22.822272754Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21kh","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:54.194943376Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-29fl","title":"acfs status: One-line health summary command","description":"## Overview
Add `acfs status` command that outputs a single-line health summary, perfect for shell prompts or quick checks.

## Current Problem
- `acfs doctor` is verbose and takes time to run
- No quick way to check "is everything OK?"
- Shell prompt integration requires parseable output

## Proposed Output Format
```bash
$ acfs status
ACFS OK: 26 tools, last update 2h ago

$ acfs status  # with issues
ACFS WARN: 2 outdated tools, doctor recommended
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: Everything healthy
- 1: Warnings (outdated tools, minor issues)
- 2: Errors (missing tools, broken state)

## Implementation Details
1. New file: `scripts/commands/status.sh`
2. Quick checks only (no network calls by default):
   - State file exists and valid
   - Required tools present in PATH
   - Last update timestamp
3. Optional `--check-updates` for network-based checks

## Machine-Readable Mode
```bash
$ acfs status --json
{"status":"ok","tools":26,"last_update":"2026-01-25T21:00:00Z","warnings":[]}
```

## Shell Prompt Integration
```bash
# Add to .bashrc
acfs_status() { acfs status --short 2>/dev/null || echo "?"; }
PS1='$(acfs_status) \w $ '
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test exit codes for all states
- [ ] Test --json output is valid JSON
- [ ] Test runs in < 100ms (no network)
- [ ] Test with missing state file
- [ ] Test with outdated state

## Files to Create
- scripts/commands/status.sh","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:26.052120972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:06:10.803804866Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29fl","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:25.805296607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2a8k","title":"ACFS update should self-update first","description":"The acfs update command should update ACFS itself (git pull) BEFORE updating any other components. Currently no self-update happens.

Critical because:
1. Bug fixes in update.sh won't apply without manual git pull
2. Security fixes in checksums.yaml won't apply
3. New tools in manifest won't be discovered

Fix: Add update_acfs_self() as FIRST operation that does git fetch/pull and re-execs if update.sh changed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:16:03.928848634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:46:31.334103730Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:46:31.334032095Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","self-update","update"]}
+{"id":"bd-2dkb","title":"Copy-to-clipboard for web wizard code blocks","description":"## Overview
Add copy-to-clipboard buttons on all code blocks in the web wizard so users can easily copy SSH commands, installation scripts, etc.

## Current Problem
- Web wizard shows many command-line snippets
- Users must manually select and copy text
- Mobile users especially struggle with text selection
- Easy to miss characters or include extra whitespace

## Proposed Solution
- Add clipboard icon button on hover/focus for all <pre>/<code> blocks
- Click copies content to clipboard
- Visual feedback: "Copied!" tooltip for 2 seconds
- Graceful fallback for browsers without Clipboard API

## Implementation Details
1. Create `CodeBlock` component in `apps/web/components/ui/`
2. Use `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` with fallback
3. Wrap all code snippets in wizard steps with this component
4. Add Tailwind styling for hover state and tooltip

## Component API
```tsx
<CodeBlock language="bash" copyable>
  curl -fsSL https://acfs.dev/install.sh | bash
</CodeBlock>
```

## Browser Support
- Modern browsers: navigator.clipboard API
- Fallback: document.execCommand('copy') with hidden textarea
- Feature detection, not browser sniffing

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test copy works in Chrome, Firefox, Safari
- [ ] Test fallback in older browsers
- [ ] Test keyboard accessibility (Enter/Space to copy)
- [ ] Visual regression test for button positioning

## Files to Create/Modify
- New: apps/web/components/ui/code-block.tsx
- Modify: All wizard step components that show code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:19.619836939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:06:00.994172740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2dkb","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:09.035245005Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2gog","title":"Add doctor.sh health checks for all utility tools","description":"## Task

Add basic health checks to scripts/lib/doctor.sh for all 9 utility tools.

## Checks to Add

Each utility should have at minimum a binary existence check:

| Tool | Check Command |
|------|---------------|
| tru | command -v tru |
| rust_proxy | command -v rust_proxy |
| rano | command -v rano |
| xf | command -v xf |
| mdwb | command -v mdwb |
| pt | command -v pt |
| aadc | command -v aadc |
| s2p | command -v s2p |
| caut | command -v caut |

## Implementation Pattern

Use the existing check() function pattern:
```bash
check "util.tru" "tru (token notation)" "$(command -v tru &>/dev/null && echo pass || echo warn)" "optional utility"
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 9 utilities have health checks
2. Checks are non-fatal (warn, not fail) since utilities are optional
3. Doctor runs without errors
4. Proper categorization in doctor output","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:33.424346589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:10:12.798643984Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","health-check","utilities"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gog","depends_on_id":"bd-1ega","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:10:12.798545559Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gog","depends_on_id":"bd-1ega.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:47.793624868Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2gys","title":"Step validation in web wizard before navigation","description":"## Overview
Add pre-navigation validation in the web wizard to ensure required fields are filled before moving to the next step.

## Current Problem
- Users can click "Next" without completing required actions
- Leads to confusion in later steps that depend on earlier choices
- No feedback about what's missing

## Proposed Behavior
1. Each step defines validation rules
2. "Next" button disabled until validation passes
3. Clear visual indication of incomplete fields
4. Helpful error messages explaining what's needed

## Validation Rules by Step
- OS Selection: Must select Ubuntu or Debian
- VPS Provider: Must select provider (optional: show comparison)
- SSH Setup: Must have valid SSH key path or generate new
- Install Options: At least one module selected
- etc.

## Implementation Details
1. Add `validate()` function to each wizard step config
2. Create `useStepValidation` hook
3. Validation runs on field change and before navigation
4. Store validation state in wizard context

## Component Changes
```tsx
// wizardSteps.ts
export interface WizardStep {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  slug: string;
  validate?: (state: WizardState) => ValidationResult;
}

interface ValidationResult {
  valid: boolean;
  errors: string[];
}
```

## UX Considerations
- Don't block exploration (allow viewing future steps)
- Clear distinction between "incomplete" and "invalid"
- Inline validation messages, not just on submit
- Persist partial progress to localStorage

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test each step's validation rules
- [ ] Test "Next" disabled when invalid
- [ ] Test error messages display correctly
- [ ] Test validation on field blur
- [ ] Test localStorage persistence of partial state

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/lib/wizardSteps.ts (add validate functions)
- apps/web/components/wizard/WizardNavigation.tsx
- apps/web/hooks/useStepValidation.ts (new)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:36.402265120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:06:46.721802486Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gys","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:43.392191184Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-33vh","title":"EPIC: Complete Removal of Legacy git_safety_guard System","description":"## Background

The old Python-based `git_safety_guard` system has been replaced by DCG (Destructive Command Guard), a high-performance Rust-based solution. However, remnants of the old system still exist in the codebase and cause confusion for users.

## Historical Context

- **December 17, 2025**: An AI agent ran `git checkout --` on files containing hours of uncommitted work. This incident made clear that AGENTS.md instructions don't prevent execution—mechanical enforcement is needed.
- **December 21, 2025**: git_safety_guard.py introduced (commit e47496c)
- **December 29, 2025**: Enhanced to 508 lines (commit 92f362c)
- **January 11, 2026**: Completely removed in favor of DCG (commit f1fd501)

## Problem Statement

Users running `acfs doctor` on older installations see:
```
⚠ WARN Git safety guard
      Fix: mkdir -p ~/.claude/hooks && cp ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py ~/.claude/hooks/ && chmod +x ~/.claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py
```

This fix command references files that NO LONGER EXIST. The check suggests copying from `~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py` but that path was removed from the repo.

## Root Cause Analysis

1. **install.sh lines 3937-3940**: Still has log messages saying 'Claude Git Safety Guard' even though it now calls configure_dcg()
2. **docs/manifest-gap-analysis.md**: References old system at lines 223 and 245
3. **User's deployed ~/.acfs/ directory**: Old doctor.sh on existing VPS installations still checks for git_safety_guard.py
4. **No migration path**: Users who installed before Jan 11, 2026 have stale checks

## Why DCG Replaced git_safety_guard

| Aspect | git_safety_guard (old) | DCG (new) |
|--------|------------------------|-----------|
| Language | Python | Rust |
| Performance | ~100ms startup | Sub-microsecond |
| Dependencies | Python runtime | Single binary |
| Features | Basic regex | SIMD-accelerated, modular packs |
| Maintenance | Part of ACFS | Dedicated project |
| Extensibility | Hardcoded | database, kubernetes, cloud packs |

## Scope

- Remove ALL git_safety_guard references from source code
- Update documentation to only reference DCG
- Ensure clean migration for existing users
- Update install.sh messaging

## Success Criteria

1. `grep -ri 'git_safety_guard' .` returns only historical .beads/ entries
2. `grep -ri 'Git Safety Guard' .` returns nothing
3. acfs doctor only checks for DCG
4. Documentation accurately reflects DCG as the sole safety mechanism
5. Existing users can upgrade cleanly without stale warnings","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:36:42.833192147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:36:42.833192147Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","dcg","epic","migration"]}
 {"id":"bd-33vh.1","title":"Update install.sh: Rename 'Git Safety Guard' references to 'DCG'","description":"## Task Overview

Update install.sh to remove all references to 'Git Safety Guard' and consistently use 'DCG' naming.

## Current State (install.sh lines 3933-3940)

```bash
# Install Claude destructive-command guard hook automatically.
#
# This is especially important because ACFS config includes "dangerous mode"
# aliases (e.g., \`cc\`) that can run commands without interactive approvals.
log_detail "Installing Claude Git Safety Guard (PreToolUse hook)"
try_step_eval "Installing Claude Git Safety Guard" \
    "TARGET_USER='$TARGET_USER' TARGET_HOME='$TARGET_HOME' '$ACFS_HOME/scripts/services-setup.sh' --install-claude-guard --yes" || \
    log_warn "Claude Git Safety Guard installation failed (optional)"
```

## Required Changes

1. **Line 3933-3936**: Update comment to reference DCG, not 'destructive-command guard hook'
2. **Line 3937**: Change `log_detail` message from 'Installing Claude Git Safety Guard' to 'Installing DCG (Destructive Command Guard)'
3. **Line 3938**: Change `try_step_eval` step name from 'Installing Claude Git Safety Guard' to 'Installing DCG hook'
4. **Line 3940**: Change `log_warn` message from 'Claude Git Safety Guard installation failed' to 'DCG hook installation failed'

## Why This Matters

- **User Confusion**: Users see 'Git Safety Guard' in install logs but DCG in doctor output
- **Consistency**: All messaging should use 'DCG' to match the actual tool name
- **Accuracy**: The underlying code already calls `configure_dcg()` so naming should match

## Verification

After changes:
```bash
grep -n 'Git Safety Guard' install.sh  # Should return nothing
grep -n 'DCG' install.sh                # Should show the updated lines
```

## Notes

- The `--install-claude-guard` flag in services-setup.sh is still used internally but doesn't need renaming as it's an internal API
- The actual functionality already uses DCG—this is purely a naming/messaging cleanup","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:37:01.131493895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:37:01.131493895Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-change","install-script"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.1","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:37:01.131493895Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Verification

 {"id":"bd-33vh.8","title":"End-to-end test: Fresh install + doctor confirms DCG, no git_safety_guard warnings","description":"## Task

Run a complete end-to-end test to verify complete git_safety_guard removal with DETAILED LOGGING.

## Test Script Location

Create: tests/e2e/test_git_safety_guard_removal.sh

## Logging Requirements

ALL tests MUST:
1. Log to timestamped file: /tmp/git_safety_guard_removal_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log
2. Structured format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [TEST_NAME] Message
3. JSON results file

## Test Environment

Use Docker-based test harness: ./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh

## Test Cases

### Test Case 1: Fresh Install Verification
```bash
# After fresh install in Docker
ls -la ~/.acfs/claude/              # Should only show settings.json
ls -la ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/ 2>&1   # Should fail (directory doesn't exist)
ls -la ~/.claude/hooks/ 2>&1        # Should fail or be empty
```

### Test Case 2: Doctor Output Verification
```bash
acfs doctor 2>&1 | tee doctor_output.txt

# Should NOT contain
! grep -i 'git.safety.guard' doctor_output.txt

# Should contain DCG check
grep -i 'DCG' doctor_output.txt
```

### Test Case 3: Install Log Verification
- Should see: 'Installing DCG' or similar
- Should NOT see: 'Git Safety Guard'

### Test Case 4: Settings.json Verification
```bash
cat ~/.claude/settings.json 2>/dev/null
# Should NOT contain 'git_safety_guard'
```

### Test Case 5: Codebase Audit
```bash
# No git_safety_guard in codebase
! grep -ri 'git_safety_guard' --include='*.sh' --include='*.ts' --include='*.md' .
```

## Expected Results Table

| Check | Expected |
|-------|----------|
| ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/ exists | NO |
| ~/.claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py exists | NO |
| acfs doctor mentions 'Git safety guard' | NO |
| acfs doctor mentions 'DCG' | YES |
| install.sh log mentions 'Git Safety Guard' | NO |
| install.sh log mentions 'DCG' | YES |

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Docker-based fresh install completes successfully
2. No git_safety_guard warnings in doctor output
3. DCG check present and passes
4. Install logs use 'DCG' terminology
5. No stale artifacts created
6. Detailed timestamped log file generated
7.  {"id":"bd-33vh.9","title":"Document migration path for existing users in CHANGELOG or release notes","description":"## Task Overview

Document the git_safety_guard → DCG migration for users who may be upgrading from older ACFS installations.

## Target Audience

Users who installed ACFS before January 11, 2026 (commit f1fd501) and may:
1. See 'Git safety guard' warnings in acfs doctor
2. Have stale git_safety_guard.py files
3. Be confused about the difference between the old and new systems

## Documentation Locations

### Option 1: Add to CHANGELOG.md (if exists)
```markdown
## [0.x.x] - 2026-01-xx

### Changed
- **BREAKING**: git_safety_guard replaced by DCG (Destructive Command Guard)

### Migration
If you see 'Git safety guard' warnings after updating:
1. Run `acfs update` to get the latest scripts
2. Remove legacy files: `rm -f ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py`
3. Install DCG: `dcg install`
4. Verify: `acfs doctor` should show DCG check, not git_safety_guard
```

### Option 2: Add migration section to README.md
```markdown
## Upgrading from Pre-January 2026 Installations

If you installed ACFS before January 11, 2026, you may have the old git_safety_guard system.
The replacement is DCG (Destructive Command Guard).

### Symptoms of Old Installation
- `acfs doctor` shows 'Git safety guard' warning
- File exists: ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py

### Migration Steps
1. Update ACFS: `acfs update --force`
2. Install DCG: `dcg install`
3. Verify: `acfs doctor` should show only DCG

The update process automatically cleans up legacy git_safety_guard files.
```

### Option 3: In-tool messaging (services-setup.sh)
When user runs `acfs services-setup`, if legacy files detected:
```
⚠️  Legacy git_safety_guard detected
   This has been replaced by DCG (Destructive Command Guard).
   
   Cleaning up old files...
   ✓ Removed ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py
   
   To configure DCG, select 'DCG' from the menu or run: dcg install
```

## Recommended Approach

Combine Options 1 and 3:
1. Document in CHANGELOG for release notes
2. Add proactive cleanup messaging in services-setup.sh

## Content Requirements

Documentation should explain:
1. **What changed**: git_safety_guard.py removed, DCG introduced
2. **Why**: Better performance (Rust vs Python), modular packs, dedicated maintenance
3. **How to migrate**: Run acfs update, install dcg
4. **How to verify**: acfs doctor shows DCG, no git_safety_guard warnings
5. **Timeline**: When this changed (January 11, 2026)

## Notes

- Keep migration docs concise - most users won't need them
- Focus on actionable steps, not history
- Link to DCG section in README for full documentation","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:38.244493576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:03.529994307Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["documentation","migration","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.9","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:38.244493576Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.9","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:03.529936278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+{"id":"bd-39ye","title":"NO_COLOR environment variable support","description":"## Overview
Respect the NO_COLOR environment variable across all ACFS scripts per https://no-color.org/ standard.

## Current Problem
- Scripts use colors unconditionally
- Users with accessibility needs can't disable colors
- Piped output includes ANSI codes
- Some terminals render colors poorly

## NO_COLOR Standard
- If NO_COLOR env var is set (any value), disable colors
- Also disable for non-TTY output (pipes, redirects)
- Simple, widely adopted convention

## Implementation Details
1. Create color helper functions in logging.sh
2. Check NO_COLOR and TTY status once at startup
3. All color output goes through these helpers

## Color Helper Functions
```bash
# scripts/lib/colors.sh
_init_colors() {
  if [[ -n "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]] || [[ ! -t 1 ]]; then
    RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' BLUE='' RESET=''
  else
    RED='\033[0;31m' GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m' BLUE='\033[0;34m' RESET='\033[0m'
  fi
}

color_print() {
  local color="$1" msg="$2"
  printf '%b%s%b\n' "${!color}" "$msg" "$RESET"
}
```

## Files to Audit
- scripts/lib/logging.sh (main color usage)
- scripts/lib/tui.sh (interactive elements)
- scripts/install.sh (progress output)
- All scripts that use printf with ANSI codes

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test NO_COLOR=1 disables all colors
- [ ] Test piped output has no ANSI codes
- [ ] Test colors work normally when NO_COLOR unset
- [ ] Grep for raw ANSI codes to ensure none slip through

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/logging.sh (add color helpers)
- All files using hardcoded ANSI codes","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:49.345301724Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:07:37.417542542Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39ye","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:01.194662910Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3aa6","title":"Prevent gcloud 'bv' from ever shadowing beads_viewer","description":"Summary
- gcloud SDK installs a `bv` binary in `/home/ubuntu/google-cloud-sdk/bin`.
- The SDK’s `path.zsh.inc` is sourced in `~/.zshrc.local`, so PATH can include gcloud’s `bv`.
- User requirement: **gcloud’s `bv` must never, under any circumstances, intercept/be invoked instead of beads_viewer `bv`.**

Impact
- High risk of running the wrong `bv` in interactive shells, non-interactive shells, CI, or cron jobs.
- Mis-executed `bv` can break bead workflows and cause confusion during incident response.

Evidence (current environment)
- `which -a bv` shows multiple `bv` binaries including gcloud’s:
  - `/home/ubuntu/google-cloud-sdk/bin/bv`
  - `/home/ubuntu/.local/bin/bv`
  - `/home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bv`
  - `/home/ubuntu/go/bin/bv`
- PATH is mutated by gcloud SDK via `path.zsh.inc` (sourced in `~/.zshrc.local`).

Root Cause
- gcloud SDK ships a `bv` command (BigQuery-related) that collides with beads_viewer’s `bv`.
- PATH ordering is not explicitly pinned to prefer user `bv` binaries.

Proposed Remediation (must enforce precedence)
1) Prepend user bins ahead of gcloud in shell init:
   - `~/bin`, `~/.local/bin`, `~/.bun/bin`, `~/go/bin` must come before the SDK.
2) Add an explicit `bv` shim at `~/bin/bv` that delegates to the preferred beads_viewer binary.
3) Add a hard alias in shell init to force `bv` -> `~/.local/bin/bv` (or preferred).
4) Add a health check command (or script) that fails if `command -v bv` resolves to gcloud.

Acceptance Criteria
- `command -v bv` resolves to user beads_viewer (not gcloud) in:
  - interactive shells
  - non-interactive shells (`zsh -lc`, cron)
- `which -a bv` shows gcloud’s `bv` only after user paths.
- Running `bv --version` matches beads_viewer’s version, not gcloud’s.

Notes
- The collision warning appears after `gcloud components update`.
- This is a safety/operations issue, not a GA4 issue.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:42:06.371685930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T00:42:06.371685930Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-3nbx","title":"acfs doctor --fix: Auto-remediation mode","description":"## Overview
Add `--fix` flag to `acfs doctor` that automatically remediates detected issues instead of just reporting them.

## Current Behavior
- `acfs doctor` runs health checks and reports problems
- User must manually fix each issue based on suggestions
- High friction for beginners who don't know HOW to fix things

## Proposed Behavior
- `acfs doctor --fix` attempts automatic remediation
- Safe fixes only: reinstall tools, fix permissions, regenerate configs
- Never destructive: won't delete user data or change system settings
- Falls back to manual instructions for unsafe operations

## Implementation Details
1. Modify `scripts/lib/doctor.sh` check functions to return remediation commands
2. Add `--fix` flag parsing to `scripts/commands/doctor.sh`
3. Each check needs: detect(), describe(), remediate()
4. Remediation functions wrapped in confirmation prompts
5. Dry-run mode: `--fix --dry-run` shows what would be done

## Safety Constraints
- NEVER delete user files
- NEVER modify ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc without backup
- Always confirm before system-level changes
- Log all remediation actions to ~/.local/share/acfs/doctor.log

## Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for each remediation function
- [ ] E2E test: break tool, run doctor --fix, verify fixed
- [ ] Test --dry-run shows correct actions
- [ ] Test unsafe operations fall back to manual

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh (add remediate functions)
- scripts/commands/doctor.sh (add --fix flag)
- New: scripts/lib/remediation.sh (shared remediation helpers)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:11.394425192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:05:33.578413645Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nbx","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:02:56.452338835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+{"id":"bd-zhdi","title":"Shell tab completion for acfs command","description":"## Overview
Add shell tab completion for the `acfs` command (bash and zsh).

## Current Problem
- Users must remember all subcommands
- No discovery mechanism for available options
- Typos in command names cause errors

## Proposed Completion
```bash
$ acfs <TAB>
doctor    install   newproj   status    update

$ acfs doctor --<TAB>
--fix     --json    --quiet   --verbose

$ acfs install <TAB>
all       core      dev       shell     # module names from manifest
```

## Implementation Details
1. Generate completions from manifest.yaml at install time
2. Support both bash and zsh
3. Install to standard locations:
   - Bash: ~/.local/share/bash-completion/completions/acfs
   - Zsh: ~/.local/share/zsh/site-functions/_acfs
4. Source automatically via shell rc files

## Completion Sources
- Static: command names (doctor, install, update, etc.)
- Dynamic: module names from manifest.yaml
- Dynamic: tool names for granular install

## Installation
```bash
# Added to install.sh
acfs_install_completions() {
  local shell="$1"
  case "$shell" in
    bash) install_bash_completions ;;
    zsh)  install_zsh_completions ;;
  esac
}
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test bash completion for all commands
- [ ] Test zsh completion for all commands
- [ ] Test dynamic module name completion
- [ ] Test completion after fresh install
- [ ] Test update preserves completions

## Files to Create
- scripts/completions/acfs.bash
- scripts/completions/_acfs (zsh)
- Modify: scripts/install.sh (install completions)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:32.204228108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:06:38.397635001Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zhdi","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:36.006705246Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
